topic: Two friends plan separate birthday parties on the same day
raw_output:
[story 1]

Liam stared at the calendar, the ink blurring under his gaze. Maya’s cheerful text message glowed on his phone: *“Can’t wait for our epic day! Cake and questionable karaoke await!”* He felt a sudden, sharp tightening in his chest, like a knot being pulled too tight. He reread the plans—his quiet dinner with his book club, Maya’s planned rooftop bash—and a cold dread washed over him. He tried to focus on the mundane task of ordering takeout, but the sounds of the city seemed amplified, every passing siren sounding like an accusation. He nervously chewed on his lip, his hands beginning to tremble slightly as he typed a reply, trying to sound casual when his insides were churning with a strange, unwelcome anxiety.

[story 2]

"So, you're sure about this?" Sarah asked, her voice pitched a little too high. She fiddled incessantly with the edge of her napkin, her eyes darting around the overly bright living room. She had spent the morning trying to reconcile the sheer absurdity of the situation: celebrating two separate milestones on the same solar rotation. A flush crept up her neck, making her skin feel suddenly too warm. Every time she looked toward the door, expecting a sudden intrusion, she found only silence, which somehow felt louder than any noise. She took a deep, shaky breath, forcing a smile that didn't quite reach her eyes, and decided she needed to move, needing distance from the suffocating feeling pressing down on her shoulders.

[story 3]

"I just… I don't know how this is going to work," Mark mumbled, running a hand repeatedly through his already messy hair. He kept catching glimpses of Maya laughing on a screen, the image mocking his own internal turmoil. A distinct pressure built behind his sternum, making it difficult to draw a full breath. He paced the small apartment three times, his footsteps echoing hollowly on the hardwood floor. He stopped abruptly by the window, staring out at the streetlights, his jaw clenched so tightly he could feel the muscles strain in his neck. He needed a distraction, anything to pull him out of this suffocating sense of imbalance before he did something impulsive.
